ddls:
CREATE TABLE "threats" (
  "threat_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each threat */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Date and time when the threat was detected */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username of the person who reported the threat */,
  "url" TEXT /* URL associated with the threat */,
  "threat_type" TEXT /* Type of threat (e.g., phishing, malware) */,
  "twitter_link" TEXT /* Link to the Twitter post reporting the threat */,
  "threat_severity" TEXT /* Severity level of the threat */,
  "threat_description" TEXT /* Brief summary of the threat */,
  "impact_score" INTEGER /* Potential impact score of the threat */,
  "incident_id" INTEGER /* ID of the related incident report */,
  "source_id" INTEGER /* ID of the threat source */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("threat_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_threats_incident_id FOREIGN KEY ("incident_id") REFERENCES incident_reports ("incident_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_threats_source_id FOREIGN KEY ("source_id") REFERENCES threat_sources ("source_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "url_categories" (
  "category_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each category */,
  "category_name" TEXT /* Name of the category (e.g., phishing, malware) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the category */,
  "category_code" TEXT /* Unique code for each category */,
  "parent_category_id" INTEGER /* Parent category ID for hierarchical categorization */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("category_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., analyst, administrator) */,
  "team_id" INTEGER /* ID of the team the user belongs to */,
  "role_description" TEXT /* Brief description of the user's role */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_users_team_id FOREIGN KEY ("team_id") REFERENCES teams ("team_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "threat_analysis" (
  "analysis_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each analysis report */,
  "threat_id" INTEGER /* ID of the threat being analyzed */,
  "analysis_date" TEXT /* Date of the analysis */,
  "analyst_id" INTEGER /* ID of the analyst conducting the analysis */,
  "report" TEXT /* Detailed report of the analysis findings */,
  "analysis_status" TEXT /* Status of the analysis (e.g., pending, in progress, completed) */,
  "conclusion" TEXT /* Summary of the analysis conclusions */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("analysis_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "alerts" (
  "alert_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each alert */,
  "threat_id" INTEGER /* ID of the threat linked to the alert */,
  "alert_type" TEXT /* Type of alert (e.g., critical, warning) */,
  "alert_date" TEXT /* Date and time the alert was generated */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who received the alert */,
  "alert_status" TEXT /* Status of the alert (e.g., active, resolved) */,
  "escalation_level" INTEGER /* Escalation level required for the alert */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("alert_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "responses" (
  "response_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each response */,
  "threat_id" INTEGER /* ID of the threat being addressed */,
  "response_date" TEXT /* Date and time of the response */,
  "response_action" TEXT /* Action taken in response to the threat */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user taking the action */,
  "response_status" TEXT /* Status of the response (e.g., pending, in progress, completed) */,
  "resolution_summary" TEXT /* Summary of the resolution actions taken */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("response_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "incident_reports" (
  "incident_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each incident report */,
  "threat_id" INTEGER /* ID of the threat related to the incident */,
  "report_date" TEXT /* Date of the incident report */,
  "description" TEXT /* Detailed description of the incident */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who generated the report */,
  "incident_status" TEXT /* Status of the incident (e.g., open, in progress, resolved) */,
  "root_cause" TEXT /* Analysis of the root cause of the incident */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("incident_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "threat_sources" (
  "source_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each threat source */,
  "source_name" TEXT /* Name of the source */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the source */,
  "source_type" TEXT /* Type of source (e.g., social media, website) */,
  "source_rating" INTEGER /* Reliability rating of the source */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("source_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "source_links" (
  "link_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each link */,
  "threat_id" INTEGER /* ID of the threat referenced */,
  "source_id" INTEGER /* ID of the source linking to the threat */,
  "link_type" TEXT /* Nature of the link between the threat and the source */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("link_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "audit_logs" (
  "log_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each log entry */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Date and time of the action */,
  "action" TEXT /* Description of the action taken */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who performed the action */,
  "log_level" TEXT /* Severity level of the log entry */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("log_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "teams" (
  "team_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each team */,
  "team_name" TEXT /* Name of the team */,
  "team_lead_id" INTEGER /* ID of the team lead */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the team */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("team_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "team_members" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user */,
  "team_id" INTEGER /* ID of the team the user belongs to */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id", "team_id")
)
